claim: Gemini 3.1 Flash Light is significantly cheaper than Gemini 3.1 Pro at around 25 cents per million tokens analyzed, yet still capable of analyzing video and audio.
actionable: Use Gemini 3.1 Flash Light for cost-sensitive video/audio analysis tasks, especially for prototyping and testing before upgrading to Pro models.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: AI Studio's Build feature can generate full-stack applications from prompts including database integration, Firebase Firestore rules, OAuth authentication, and image processing all automatically.
actionable: Use AI Studio Build feature when you need rapid prototyping of full-stack apps with authentication and database storage; click 'get code' to export working code in Python/TypeScript.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: When models receive context windows too small for your needs, vector databases become unnecessary workarounds as context windows expand—models now support 100k+ tokens making many workarounds obsolete.
actionable: Before building vector database solutions for long context problems, verify your target model's actual context window; focus on retrieval only when context limitations persist after model upgrades.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemini models are natively multimodal and can output multiple modalities (text, code, images, audio tokens interleaved) unlike most competitors that only output text and code.
actionable: Leverage Gemini's multimodal output capabilities for complex workflows requiring mixed media generation; avoid sequential API calls to different services.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemini Live enables real-time conversation with video/screen sharing in multiple languages and supports switching language mid-conversation through system instructions.
actionable: Use Gemini Live for real-time interactive demos requiring video feeds or screen sharing; request 'get code' after a successful interaction to replicate in your own applications.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Genie 3 world model generates playable pixel-by-pixel environments frame-by-frame without storing 3D assets or physics engines; objects persist during interactions.
actionable: Use Genie 3 for rapid prototyping of interactive game worlds and simulations; understand it generates raw pixels rather than 3D geometry, limiting precision of physics-based interactions.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Nano Banana 2 supports search grounding and reverse image search to generate images based on web-found references, improving consistency and real-world accuracy.
actionable: Use Nano Banana 2 with search grounding when generating images requiring real-world accuracy (e.g., landmarks, specific objects); enable reverse image search for visual style consistency.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: VO 3.1 Light is the cheapest video generation model at 5 cents per image, enabling cost-effective prototyping before upgrading to higher-quality VO 3 models.
actionable: Start video generation projects with VO 3.1 Light for prompt testing and iteration; upgrade to VO 3 only after confirming quality requirements justify higher costs.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: LIA 3 is Google's first API-accessible music generation model supporting both 30-second and full 3-minute songs with lyrics.
actionable: Use LIA 3 API for agent-based music generation workflows; design prompts describing tempo, genre, mood, and instrumentation for consistent results.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: LIA Real Time generates music indefinitely with real-time prompt changes like a DJ, offering continuous generative creativity without discrete song boundaries.
actionable: Use LIA Real Time for interactive music experiences and dynamic soundscaping; prompt changes in real-time to transition between musical themes.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gen media models use retry logic during initialization which helps with rate-limiting issues, especially when regions wake up (e.g., US daytime causing Nano Banana capacity strain).
actionable: Implement client-side retry logic when initializing gen media model clients; increase retry attempts during peak usage hours in populous regions.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Structured outputs with gen media models ensure predictable function returns (just the prompt, no preamble) rather than accepting natural language responses that may include introductory text.
actionable: Use structured outputs when chaining gen media model calls to avoid parsing natural language responses; define exact return schemas for each step.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Chat mode preserves model history across requests, allowing models to maintain context about previously uploaded files without re-uploading, improving efficiency.
actionable: Use chat mode for multi-turn workflows involving large file uploads (e.g., books, PDFs); upload once and reference in subsequent prompts within the same session.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Nano Banana benefits from system instructions to prevent unwanted features like titles and borders on book-related images; 'stay family friendly' prevents inappropriate content.
actionable: Add explicit negative system instructions to Nano Banana prompts to prevent default behaviors (borders, titles); test image model versions as they vary in compliance.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Passing reference images of characters explicitly to image generation models improves consistency; limiting reference images per generation request (not showing all possibilities) prevents model confusion.
actionable: When generating character variations in images, pass only the relevant reference images for that specific scene; avoid showing models all possible character images simultaneously.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Text-to-speech models require explicit prompts like 'read this' or 'tell me this'—sending raw text alone gets ignored; system context about accents and emotions goes in these prompts.
actionable: Always prefix TTS requests with 'read this' or 'tell me this'; embed emotional context and accent instructions in these prompt prefixes.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Different character voices in text-to-speech can be simulated by using consistent voice aliases paired with specific prompting styles; same 'character' named with same prompt style produces consistent voice.
actionable: Reuse the same voice alias for recurring characters with consistent prompt styling (e.g., 'character speaks quickly with British accent') to create multi-voice narration.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Vertex AI provides data residency controls for Europe compliance (data stays within Europe); requires more setup overhead and is recommended only for teams with DevOps expertise or existing GCP infrastructure.
actionable: Use Vertex AI for enterprise deployments with strict data residency requirements; use AI Studio for rapid prototyping unless you already have GCP infrastructure.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemma 4 E2B model runs on phones and Raspberry Pi with 2B effective parameters (embeddings stored in flash, paged as needed) matching 5-8B dense model capabilities.
actionable: Use Gemma 4 E2B for on-device inference on phones and edge devices; understand effective parameters differ from parameter count due to embedding storage architecture.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemma 4 26B is a mixture-of-experts model with 4B activated parameters requiring only 7-18GB RAM for inference (vs 31B dense model's higher RAM), offering better speed than dense models of similar capacity.
actionable: Choose Gemma 4 26B over 31B for laptops with limited RAM; accepts the tradeoff of slight capability reduction for significant speed improvements.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Agent skills in Gemma on device enable function calling for Android intents and custom JavaScript skills, allowing models to trigger other apps or run web views directly on phones.
actionable: Use agent skills in Gemma mobile apps to let models call native Android intents and custom JavaScript functions; design skill descriptions precisely for accurate function selection.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemma 4 on OpenAI-compatible endpoints (served via LM Studio on port 1234) can be used with any client library expecting OpenAI API schema by changing just the base URL.
actionable: Serve Gemma 4 locally on OpenAI-compatible endpoint; change only the API base URL in existing OpenAI client code to use local model instead of cloud.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Orchestrating 10 parallel sub-agents locally using Gemma 4 26B generates SVGs concurrently with combined token throughput visible; suitable for subdivision of tasks like file sorting, code implementation, research analysis.
actionable: Design agentic workflows using Gemma 4 26B to parallelize independent subtasks; monitor combined token throughput to optimize batch sizing.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Enabling thinking in Gemma 4 models improves output quality at the cost of additional computation; thinking adds a planning step before code execution.
actionable: Enable thinking mode in Gemma 4 for complex tasks requiring reasoning (code generation, problem solving); disable for speed-critical applications.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemma 4 31B is superior to 26B for understanding ambiguous task specs and autonomously spawning sub-agents without explicit prompting.
actionable: Use 31B for complex spec interpretation and autonomous agent spawning; use 26B when you can provide explicit task subdivisions to sub-agents.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Gemma models loaded in LM Studio can be served on local OpenAI-compatible endpoints to other applications like Open Code for code generation tasks.
actionable: Configure LM Studio to serve Gemma on localhost:1234 with OpenAI API schema; point Open Code or other IDEs to this endpoint for local code generation.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Errors during agentic code generation can be fed back to the model iteratively; models can re-examine file systems and regenerate files, debug line-by-line, or fully rewrite problematic code.
actionable: When code generation fails, ask models to examine error messages and reread relevant files before regenerating; provide specific file paths for targeted edits.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack

claim: Models performing vision-based web page recreation benefit from explicit multimodal input (screenshot upload) combined with text description for better layout/styling preservation.
actionable: When asking models to recreate web pages, provide both a screenshot and the URL or description; models better preserve visual hierarchy and styling.
source_title: Prompt to Pipeline: Building with Google's Gen Media Stack
